title: Unit Cost: Absorption vs Variable Costing
event: Reyes Co.'s product costs per unit: direct materials $6, direct labor $4, variable overhead $2. Fixed overhead is $60,000 for 10,000 units produced. Compute the unit product cost under the assumed method.

computationPaths.narration: Absorption costing treats fixed overhead as a product cost — every unit carries its $6 share.
Variable costing sends fixed overhead straight to the period — units carry only what varies with production.

steps.label: Fixed OH
steps.formulaText: 60,000 expensed as a lump — none attaches to units

memorize.body: Absorption unit cost = DM + DL + VOH + (fixed OH ÷ units PRODUCED). Variable unit cost = DM + DL + VOH only.
The entire dispute is one item: fixed overhead. Product cost (absorption) vs period cost (variable).

questions.prompt: DM $6, DL $4, VOH $2, fixed OH $60,000 ÷ 10,000 units. ABSORPTION unit cost = ?

distractors.feedback: That's VARIABLE costing. Absorption adds the $6 fixed-OH share: 18.
You doubled the fixed OH or added selling costs. Product cost stops at DM + DL + VOH + fixed OH: 18.
